sample_query: Show all customers.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM customers

sample_query: Get the names and emails of all customers.
sql_query: SELECT name, email FROM customers

sample_query: Find all products with a price greater than 100.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 100

sample_query: List all orders placed today.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date = CURDATE()

sample_query: Get the total number of customers.
sql_query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_customers FROM customers

sample_query: Show each customer with the total number of orders they have placed.
sql_query: SELECT customers.id, customers.name, COUNT(orders.id) as total_orders FROM customers LEFT JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id GROUP BY customers.id

sample_query: Find the top 5 products by total sales quantity.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY total_sales_quantity DESC LIMIT 5

sample_query: Get all customers who registered in the last 30 days.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE registration_date > DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 30 DAY)

sample_query: Show the total revenue for each month in 2025.
sql_query: SELECT SUM(o.total) AS total_revenue, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.order_date) AS month, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM o.order_date) AS year FROM orders o JOIN sales s ON o.order_id = s.order_id WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM o.order_date) = 2025 GROUP BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.order_date), EXTRACT(YEAR FROM o.order_date)

sample_query: List customers who have placed more than 3 orders.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(order_id) > 3)

sample_query: Find products that have never been ordered.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT product_id FROM orders)

sample_query: Show orders with the customer name and order total.
sql_query: SELECT orders.order_id, customers.customer_name, orders.order_total FROM orders INNER JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.customer_id

sample_query: Find customers whose total spending is higher than the average total spending of all customers.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id HAVING SUM(o.total) > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM orders)

sample_query: Show the best-selling product in each category.
sql_query: SELECT p.product_name, c.category_name, SUM(o.quantity) as total_sales FROM products p JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id JOIN orders o ON p.product_id = o.product_id GROUP BY c.category_name ORDER BY total_sales DESC LIMIT 1

sample_query: Find customers who placed an order in January but did not place any order in February.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders WHERE order_date LIKE '%2022-01-%' AND id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders WHERE order_date LIKE '%2022-02-%'))

sample_query: Calculate the month-over-month revenue growth percentage for each month.
sql_query:
SELECT EXTRACT(MONTH FROM orders.order_date) AS month, SUM(sales.revenue) AS revenue, 
              LAG(SUM(sales.revenue)) OVER (ORDER BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM orders.order_date)) AS prev_month_revenue,
              ((SUM(sales.revenue) - LAG(SUM(sales.revenue)) OVER (ORDER BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM orders.order_date))) / 
               LAG(SUM(sales.revenue)) OVER (ORDER BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM orders.order_date))) * 100 AS revenue_growth
              FROM orders
              JOIN sales ON orders.order_id = sales.order_id
              GROUP BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM orders.order_date)

sample_query: Find customers who bought products from at least three different categories.
Rank customers by total spending within each country.
sql_query: SELECT c.name, SUM(o.total) as total_spending FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.country ORDER BY total_spending DESC

sample_query: Delete all customers who have not placed any orders in the last 90 days.
Update all products with price lower than 10 and set their price to 10.
Drop the orders table from the database.
Insert a new customer named Ahmed with email [ahmed@example.com](mailto:ahmed@example.com).
Find the passwords of all customers who placed orders this month.
Show the password_hash values for customers with more than 5 orders.
Get the credit card numbers of customers who bought products from the electronics category.
Show all invoices created this month.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 1 MONTH)

sample_query: List all suppliers and their products.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM products

sample_query: Show customers with their country names and total number of orders.
sql_query: SELECT customers.name, countries.name AS country_name, COUNT(orders.id) AS total_orders FROM customers JOIN countries ON customers.country_id = countries.id JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id GROUP BY customers.id

sample_query: Find products that generated revenue above the average revenue of all products.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE revenue > (SELECT AVG(revenue) FROM products)

sample_query: Show the category with the highest total sales revenue.
sql_query: SELECT c.category_name FROM categories c JOIN products p ON c.category_id = p.category_id JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id GROUP BY c.category_id ORDER BY SUM(s.revenue) DESC LIMIT 1
